docs.title: LiveDaten
docs.text:
Die LiveDaten-Ansicht bietet eine Echtzeit-Visualisierung der Knüppelwege und Schaltzustände. Links unten kann die Kanalzuordnung angepasst werden, insbesondere bei Verwendung von IBUS, SBUS oder SUMD. Kanäle können hier auch einzeln eingelernt werden.

Zum Einlernen klicken Sie auf den Button „Einlernen“ und folgen den Anweisungen.

image45.png

Die rechte Seite der Ansicht passt sich je nach Steuerpad oder SUMD-Fernsteuerung an.

image46.png

docs.title: Geräteinformationen
docs.text:
Hier werden alle modulspezifischen Informationen angezeigt, darunter:

  • Name des Moduls
  • Software-Version
  • Version der Soundengine
  • Bootloader-Version
  • Anzahl der Ausgänge
  • Produktionsdatum
  • Gerätetyp

docs.title: Systemeinstellungen
docs.text:
Grundlegende Einstellungen, die das Verhalten des Modells maßgeblich beeinflussen:

image47.png

  • Name: Bezeichnung des Moduls in der Übersicht, wichtig bei mehreren KLB-Modulen
  • Einlernmodus aktiv: Manuelle Einlernprozedur kann hier deaktiviert werden
  • Abschaltspannung: 6,4 V für 7,2 V NiMh/NiCd Akkus, 9,6 V für 3S LiPo
  • Modellnummer: Umschaltung zwischen drei Modellen
  • Kurze/Lange Betätigung: Definiert, ab wann ein Tastendruck als lang gilt
  • Lautstärke: Startlautstärke des Sounds, passt sich automatisch an Poti oder Knüppelweg an
  • Halbausschlag und Vollausschlag: Schwellwerte für Ausschläge
  • Infrarot-Sendeleistung: Einstellung je nach Abstand der Dioden (1 = klein, 3 = groß)
  • Motorprofil: Beeinflusst Anfahrverhalten und Tempomat
  • Motorstart automatisch: Motor startet bei leichtem Vorwärtsbewegung des Knüppels
  • Motorstop nach: Zeit in Sekunden bis Motorstop bei Inaktivität
  • Tagfahrlicht immer an: Schaltet Tagfahrlicht vor Standlicht ein
  • Blinker Ein/Aus: Pulsdauer für Blinkgeschwindigkeit
  • Fahrtenreglertyp: Aktuell nur „Bremse getrennt“ unterstützt
  • Rückwärtsfahren: Kopplung von Warnblinkern und Abbiegelichtern
  • Bremslicht Sensitivität und Nachleuchten
  • Rückfahrlicht Nachleuchten
  • Lenkungstyp: Automatische oder manuelle Blinkerbetätigung
  • Blinker Schwellwert: Ab wann Blinker geschaltet oder zurückgesetzt werden
  • Kurvenlichttyp: Immer an oder nur bei Fahrlicht
  • Motor läuft sofort: Motor startet sofort nach Einschalten oder manuell
  • Partymodus: Definiert Funktionen bei Tastendruck am Steuerpad
  • Pad Licht & Sound: Feinabstimmung der Steuerpad-Kanäle
  • Hydraulikdrehzahlerhöhung und Nachlauf: Einstellungen für Hydraulikkanal

docs.title: Belegung K1 – K32
docs.text:
image48.png

  • Keine: Kanal wird nicht verwendet
  • Pad Licht & Sound: Verwendung des Steuerpads an diesem Kanal
  • Benutzerdefiniert: Eigene Belegung für halbe/volle, kurze/lange Ausschläge als Memory- oder Direktfunktion

docs.title: Mögliche Einstellungen
docs.text:
image49.png

image50.png

docs.title: Weitere Funktionen
docs.text:
• Soundwechsel: Per Tastendruck zwischen Sounds wechseln
  • Modellwahlschalter: Umschalten zwischen Modellen (abhängig von Systemeinstellungen)
  • StandBy-Schalter: StandBy-Modus analog zum Schalter am RS1
  • Lautstärke: Direkte Lautstärkeregelung über Fernsteuerung
  • Hydraulik: Löst Drehzahlerhöhung des Motorsounds aus

docs.title: RS1 Soundeinstellungen
docs.text: image51.png

docs.title: Grundeinstellungen
docs.text:
image52.png

  • Projektname: Auswahl der Projekte und mitgelieferten Sounds
  • Drehzahlbereich Min/Max: Definiert den Sound-Drehzahlbereich des Motors
  • Dynamik: Bestimmt, wie agil der Sound auf Fahrzustände reagiert
  • Motorcharakteristik: Maximalgeschwindigkeit für Beschleunigung und Auslaufen
  • Turbocharakteristik: Maximalgeschwindigkeit für Turbo-Beschleunigung und Auslaufen
  • Motorbremse Umschaltgeschwindigkeit %: Übergangsgeschwindigkeit des Sounds zwischen Normalzustand und Last
  • Standardtyp: Vorauswahl des Soundtyps bei neuem Soundeintrag
  • Last Umschaltgeschwindigkeit %: Übergangsgeschwindigkeit zwischen Normalzustand und Motorbremse

image53.png

image54.png

Hinweis: Die Sounds am PC sind aus Performance- und Copyright-Gründen in reduzierter Qualität. Auf dem Modul erfolgt die Wiedergabe in voller Qualität.

docs.title: Soundsimulation am PC
docs.text:
image55.png

Nach Klick auf „Motor Start“ wird der Startsound abgespielt und der Motorsound beginnt zu laufen.

image56.png

Der linke Regler steuert die Drehzahl: nach oben für Beschleunigung, nach unten für Ausdrehen bzw. Motorbremse.

image56.png

Die Balken zwei bis vier visualisieren Drehzahl und Beschleunigung, sind jedoch nicht direkt veränderbar. Ein Marker zeigt den aktuell aktiven Sound und die Lautstärke an.

image57.png

image58.png

docs.title: Einstellungen speichern
docs.text:
• Grundeinstellungen speichern
  • Sounds speichern
  • SD-Karte synchronisieren (setzt alle Soundeinstellungen zurück)

docs.title: Soundtypen
docs.text:
image59.png

  • Motorsound Standard: Beschleunigung und kontinuierliche Fahrt
  • Motorsound Last: Beschleunigung und Fahrt im Lastmodus
  • Motorsound Verzögerung: Fahrzeug wird langsamer
  • Motorsound Motorbremse: Fahrzeug wird langsamer mit Motorbremse
  • Motorsound Lastwechsel: Einmaliger Sound beim Fahrzustandswechsel
  • Motor Start/Stop: Start- und Stoppsound, lastabhängig schaltbar
  • Motor Fehlstart: Fehlstart-Sound
  • Turbo: Turbopfeifen
  • Schaltgeräusch: Sound beim Gangwechsel
  • Luftbremse An/Aus: Sounds der Druckluftbremse
  • Rückfahrwarner: Bei Rückwärtsfahrt
  • Hupe/Horn/Fanfare: Über Hupe schaltbar
  • Zusatzsound: Benutzerdefinierte Sounds
  • Einschaltsound: Nach vollständigem Start des Moduls
  • Anfahrsound: Beim Anfahren (meist nicht nötig)
  • Anhaltesound: Beim Anhalten
  • MP3: Nur ein MP3 gleichzeitig abspielbar

docs.title: Soundeinstellungen Motorsounds
docs.text:
image60.png

image61.png

  • Vorauswahl Motorsounds: Standard, Last, Verzögerung, Motorbremse
  • Dateiname auf SD-Karte: .snd ist das interne Kraftwerk-Format
  • Play: Sound abspielen
  • Öffnen: Sounddatei laden
  • Volume: Grundlautstärke, abhängig von Fahrsituation
  • Häkchen: Definieren, in welchen Fahrzuständen der Sound aktiv ist

image62.png

docs.title: Visualisierung des Sounds
docs.text:
image63.png

  • Min RPM: Drehzahl, ab der der Sound langsam eingeblendet wird
  • Ref RPM: Basisdrehzahl, bei der der Sound mit voller Lautstärke abgespielt wird
  • Max RPM: Drehzahl, bis zu der der Sound langsam ausgeblendet wird

Die Sounds werden drehzahlangepasst ein- und ausgeblendet, was ein realistisches Klangbild erzeugt.

docs.title: Zusatzsounds
docs.text:
image64.png

Die linke Hälfte entspricht den Motorsounds mit Soundtyp, Name, Play, Öffnen und Lautstärke. Unterschied: Nur zwei Häkchen verfügbar (Motor Standard und Motor Last).

image65.png

docs.title: Obere Buttons
docs.text:
• Auslöser: 1. Steigende Flanke, 2. Fallende Flanke, 3. Beide Flanken
  • Abspieltyp: 1. Schleife, 2. Immer abspielen, 3. Zurück zum Anfang oder weiterspielen

Alle Kombinationen sind möglich.

docs.title: Auslösende Funktion
docs.text:
Je nach Verwendung von Steuerpad oder SUMD-Fernsteuerung stehen unterschiedliche Schalter zur Verfügung. Diese entsprechen den Funktionen in den LiveDaten.

image66.png

image67.png

image68.png

docs.title: Stopps
docs.text:
Für jeden Sound können Stopppunkte in Sekunden definiert werden. Bei aktivierter „immer abspielen“-Funktion läuft der Sound bis zum nächsten Stopppunkt oder weiter, falls die Funktion aktiv bleibt.

image69.png

docs.title: Zufall
docs.text:
Der Sound startet alle x bis y Sekunden, sofern er aktiv ist.

image70.png

docs.title: Neuen Sound hinzufügen
docs.text:
Unterstützte Soundtypen:

  • Alle gängigen Audio- und Videoformate werden unterstützt.
  • Das ControlPanel konvertiert automatisch in das benötigte interne Format inklusive Bitrate.
  • Beispiele: WAV, MP3, OGG, MP4, SND

Am Ende der Soundliste befindet sich der Button „Neuer Soundeintrag“:

image71.png

Nach Klick wird eine neue Zeile mit dem Standard-Soundtyp aus den Grundeinstellungen angelegt. Mit „Öffnen“ kann die Datei geladen werden.

Alternativ können Dateien per Drag & Drop in das ControlPanel gezogen werden:

image72.png

image73.png

Wird eine Datei in die Soundvisualisierung gezogen, ersetzt sie den alten Sound.

Für Motorsounds können mehrere Dateien gleichzeitig importiert werden. Enthalten die Dateinamen eine dreibis fünfstellige Zahl, wird diese als „ref RPM“ übernommen und die Drehzahlbereiche automatisch angepasst. Diese können später im ControlPanel optimiert und auf das Modul geladen werden.

image74.png

docs.title: Anhang: Namensgebung
docs.text: Kraftwerk Typ Master/Busknoten Ein/Ausgänge Zusatzinformation R S 1 5 Servoeingänge / 5 Servoausgänge Regler / Sound K L M 4/16 Kraftwerk Licht Master, 4 Servoeingänge / 16 Schaltausgänge K L B 8 Kraftwerk Licht Master, 4 Servoeingänge / 12 Schaltausgänge, 500 mA pro Ausgang K S B 2 Kraftwerk Licht Busknoten, 500 mA pro Ausgang K M B 1 Kraftwerk Servo Busknoten, 2 Ausgänge, 10 A Kraftwerk Motor Busknoten, 1 Ausgang, 10 Ampere

docs.title: Begriffe
docs.text:
• ControlPanel: Kostenfreie PC-Software zur Konfiguration des Systems.
  • Steuerpad: Am Fernsteuersender montiertes Bedienfeld zur komfortablen Steuerung des Systems.
  • Parameter: Veränderbare Werte oder Einstellungen im System.

docs.title: Anleitung zur Installation der Premiumsounds
docs.text: Wolfgang Haring e.U. Firmenbuchnummer: FN 312421 v (Landesgericht Wr. Neustadt) Kammanngasse 7-9A/8, A-2700 Wiener Neustadt Telefon: +49 30 6098490 431

docs.title: Grundlegendes
docs.text: Im Folgenden wird der Begriff „Soundmodul“ verwendet. Damit sind alle Module gemeint, die die KraftwerK Soundengine nutzen. Aktuell betrifft dies die Modelle RS1, ST1 und SBM1.

docs.title: Bestellung der Premiumsounds
docs.text:
Premiumsounds können bequem über unseren Online-Shop bestellt werden. Im Reiter „Sounds“ finden Sie die aktuelle Auswahl an verfügbaren Premiumsounds.

image1.png

Wichtig: Jeder Sound kann nur auf einem einzelnen Soundmodul installiert werden. Falls Sie den gleichen Sound auf mehreren Modulen verwenden möchten, bieten wir attraktive Preisstaffeln an, um Ihnen Kosten zu sparen.

image2.png

Die Bestellung erfolgt wie gewohnt über den Warenkorb und wird nach Abschluss der Bezahlung automatisch bearbeitet. Es fallen keine Versandkosten an.

docs.title: Installation am Soundmodul
docs.text:
1. Vorbereitung: Stellen Sie sicher, dass Ihr Soundmodul eingeschaltet, betriebsbereit und mit dem PC verbunden ist.
  2. Das Soundmodul benötigt mindestens die Firmware-Version 2024.3 oder höher.
  3. Nach dem Start wird das Modul automatisch erkannt und im Programm angezeigt.

image3.png

docs.title: Soundeinstellungen öffnen
docs.text:
Öffnen Sie die Soundeinstellungen im Programm.

image4.png

Klicken Sie anschließend auf den Button „Premiumsounds“, um den Premiumsoundmanager zu starten.

image5.png

image6.png

docs.title: Anmeldung und Auswahl der Sounds
docs.text:
Geben Sie Ihre Benutzerdaten ein, mit denen Sie die Premiumsounds gekauft haben. Nach dem Klick auf „Anmelden“ werden alle Ihre Käufe angezeigt.

image7.png

In der rechten Spalte sehen Sie den Status der Lizenz:

  • „Verfügbar“: Der Sound ist noch keinem Soundmodul zugeordnet und kann installiert werden.
  • Seriennummer: Der Sound ist bereits einem Soundmodul zugeordnet.

Wählen Sie den gewünschten Sound aus, wählen Sie das passende Projekt auf der SD-Karte aus und klicken Sie auf „Download auf das Modul“.

Im Beispiel unten ist das Modul bereits registriert, der Sound soll aber erneut installiert werden, z. B. weil die SD-Karte getauscht wurde.

image8.png

docs.title: Download bestätigen
docs.text:
Bestätigen Sie den Download, um den Vorgang zu starten.

image9.png

Nach der Bestätigung wird der ausgewählte Sound heruntergeladen. Die Lizenzdatei wird entweder neu erzeugt oder wiederverwendet und anschließend zusammen mit dem Sound auf das Soundmodul kopiert.

image10.png

docs.title: Abschluss der Installation
docs.text:
Nach dem Kopiervorgang wird der Sound automatisch neu geladen.

image11.png

Der Sound ist nun einsatzbereit und kann sofort verwendet werden.

image12.png

docs.title: Hinweise und Tipps
docs.text:
• Firmware-Update: Stellen Sie sicher, dass Ihr Soundmodul immer mit der neuesten Firmware-Version betrieben wird, um Kompatibilitätsprobleme zu vermeiden.
  • Mehrfachinstallation: Wenn Sie einen Sound auf mehreren Modulen nutzen möchten, prüfen Sie vor der Bestellung die verfügbaren Preisstaffeln.
  • SD-Karte wechseln: Beim Austausch der SD-Karte muss der Sound erneut installiert werden, wie in Schritt 4 beschrieben.
  • Support: Bei Problemen oder Fragen steht Ihnen unser Kundenservice telefonisch oder per E-Mail zur Verfügung.

docs.title: Hinweise zur Fehlerbehebung
docs.text:
• Stellen Sie sicher, dass die Firmware-Version des Soundmoduls aktuell ist (mindestens 2024.3).
  • Prüfen Sie die USB-Verbindung zwischen PC und Soundmodul, falls das Modul nicht erkannt wird.
  • Falls der Sound nicht installiert werden kann, überprüfen Sie, ob die Lizenz bereits einem anderen Modul zugeordnet ist.
  • Bei Problemen mit der Anmeldung im Premiumsoundmanager kontrollieren Sie Ihre Zugangsdaten und die Internetverbindung.

docs.title: Zusätzliche Tipps
docs.text:
• Bewahren Sie Ihre Benutzerdaten sicher auf, um jederzeit Zugriff auf Ihre gekauften Sounds zu haben.
  • Nutzen Sie die Preisstaffeln, wenn Sie mehrere Module mit denselben Sounds ausstatten möchten, um Kosten zu sparen.
  • Bei Austausch der SD-Karte oder bei Modulwechsel können Sounds problemlos erneut installiert werden, solange die Lizenz gültig ist.

docs.title: RCMultiBus – Übersicht und Funktionsweise
docs.text: Der RCMultiBus dient als übergeordnetes Protokoll für alle 3-Draht-basierten Datenbusse, die im RC-Bereich verwendet werden. Ein Slave-Gerät, das RCMultiBus unterstützt, muss in der Lage sein, alle Bus-Typen inklusive Protokoll, Baudrate und Polarität zu interpretieren. Ein Master-Gerät hingegen muss nur einen der Bus-Typen erzeugen können.

docs.title: Unterstützte Bus-Typen
docs.text:
• IBUS
  • SBUS
  • EX-Bus
  • CPPM

Je nach Bus-Typ können proportionale und/oder digitale Informationen empfangen werden. Jeder proportionale Kanal wird stets in digitale Schaltfunktionen (permanent und im Speicher) umgewandelt.

docs.title: Basisfunktionen (Tag 0x01)
docs.text:
Basisfunktionen sind in 5 Bytes codiert:

Offset Bit 7 Bit 6 Bit 5 Bit 4 Bit 3 Bit 2 Bit 1 Bit 0 0 Warnblinker Blinker links Blinker rechts Fernlichtblitzer Nebelscheinwerfer Fernlicht Abblendlicht Standlicht 1 Stützbeine hoch Fünfte Rad Motor läuft Starten Hupen Hecknebellicht Bremslicht Rückfahrlicht 2 Blinker rechts Impuls Rundumleuchte Tagfahrlicht Abbiegelicht links Abbiegelicht rechts Mulde runter Mulde hoch Stützbeine runter 3 Intern/RFU Signalhorn Gang runter Gang hoch Kurvenlicht links Kurvenlicht rechts Intern/RFU Blinker links Impuls 4 Servo 2 Servo 1 Gang 2/2 Gang 1/2 Pad-Level Rampe runter Rampe hoch Intern/RFU

docs.title: Zusatzfunktionen (Tag 0x05)
docs.text: Offset Bit 7 Bit 6 Bit 5 Bit 4 Bit 3 Bit 2 Bit 1 Bit 0 0 CH1 Memory Half Short Rechts CH1 Memory Half Short Links CH1 Rechts Voll CH1 Links Voll CH1 Memory Voll Lang Rechts CH1 Memory Voll Lang Links CH1 Memory Voll Kurz Rechts CH1 Memory Voll Kurz Links 1 CH2 Memory Voll Lang Rechts CH2 Memory Voll Lang Links CH2 Memory Voll Kurz Rechts CH2 Memory Voll Kurz Links CH1 Rechts Halb CH1 Links Halb CH1 Memory Half Lang Rechts CH1 Memory Half Lang Links 2 CH2 Rechts Halb CH2 Links Halb CH2 Memory Half Lang Rechts CH2 Memory Half Lang Links CH2 Memory Half Kurz Rechts CH2 Memory Half Kurz Links CH2 Rechts Voll CH2 Links Voll … … 45 CH31 Memory Half Short Rechts CH31 Memory Half Short Links CH31 Rechts Voll CH31 Links Voll CH31 Memory Voll Lang Rechts CH31 Memory Voll Lang Links CH31 Memory Voll Kurz Rechts CH31 Memory Voll Kurz Links 46 CH32 Memory Voll Lang Rechts CH32 Memory Voll Lang Links CH32 Memory Voll Kurz Rechts CH32 Memory Voll Kurz Links CH31 Rechts Halb CH31 Links Halb CH31 Memory Half Lang Rechts CH31 Memory Half Lang Links 47 CH32 Rechts Halb CH32 Links Halb CH32 Memory Half Lang Rechts CH32 Memory Half Lang Links CH32 Memory Half Kurz Rechts CH32 Memory Half Kurz Links CH32 Rechts Voll CH32 Links Voll

docs.title: Proportionale Funktionen (Tag 0x41)
docs.text:
Die Werte sind 16-Bit vorzeichenbehaftete Werte im Bereich von -500 bis +500, was einer Anzeige von -100 % bis +100 % entspricht. Die Daten liegen im Little-Endian-Format vor.

CH1 (Gas) CH2 (Lenkung) CH3 CH4 … CH31 CH32

docs.title: Kanaldaten und Bewegungsüberwachung
docs.text:
Jeder Kanalwert wird gespeichert und kann für jeden Servo- oder Motor-Ausgang individuell zugeordnet werden. Zusätzlich wird die Bewegung jedes Kanals überwacht, woraus ein Bitfeld generiert wird. Dieses Bitfeld hat drei Dimensionen:

  • Richtung: oben (top) / unten (bottom)
  • Bewegungsintensität: voll (full) / halb (half)
  • Status: permanent / im Speicher (memory)
CH Memory Short Memory Long Permanent Top Full Top Half Bottom Half Bottom Full CH1 Memory Short Memory Long Permanent Top Full Top Half Bottom Half Bottom Full CH2 Memory Short Memory Long Permanent Top Full Top Half Bottom Half Bottom Full … CHx Memory Short Memory Long Permanent Top Full Top Half Bottom Half Bottom Full

docs.title: Beispiele zur Bewegungsüberwachung
docs.text:
• Beispiel 1: Kanal 4 wird auf +30% bewegt → Flag CH4 Permanent/Top/Half wird gesetzt.
  • Beispiel 2: Kanal 4 wird innerhalb von 0,5 Sekunden zurückbewegt → Flag CH4 Permanent/Top/Half wird gelöscht, Flag CH4 Memory/Top/Half wird gesetzt.
  • Beispiel 3: Kanal 1 wird auf -100% bewegt → Flag CH1 Permanent/Bottom/Full wird gesetzt.
  • Beispiel 4: Kanal 1 wird nach einer Sekunde zurückbewegt → Flag CH1 Permanent/Bottom/Full wird gelöscht, Flag CH1 Memory/Bottom/Full wird gesetzt.

docs.title: Kommandos
docs.text:
Alle Kommandos beginnen mit 0xA1 oder 0xA2, sind als Tag-Length-Value (TLV) kodiert und enden mit einer XOR-Prüfsumme.

Startbyte Tag (Kommando) Länge Wert (Daten) Prüfsumme 0xA1 / 0xA2 0x6C 0x03 0x01 0x01 0x01 0xCF

Die Prüfsumme wird als XOR über alle Bytes mit 0xAA als Startwert berechnet.

docs.title: SET_FCT_DIRECT – Direkte Funktionssteuerung
docs.text:
Mit dieser Funktion können einzelne Funktionen direkt ein- oder ausgeschaltet werden.

Startbyte Tag (Kommando) Länge Wert (Daten) Prüfsumme 0xA1 0x6C 0x03 0x01 0x03 0x01 0xCD

docs.title: Datenformat
docs.text: Byte 1 Byte 2 Byte 3 Datenobjekt (z. B. Basisfunktionen) Bit-Offset (1–40, z. B. Parklicht, Abblendlicht) Ein/Aus (1 = Ein, 0 = Aus)

docs.title: SET_PARAMETER – Parameteränderung
docs.text:
Mit dieser Funktion können Skriptparameter für alle Ausgänge geändert werden, z. B. „Alle Fernlicht-Helligkeitswerte auf 80 % setzen“.

Startbyte Tag (Kommando) Länge Wert (Daten) Prüfsumme 0xA1 0x76 0x02 0x04 0x50 0x81

docs.title: SET_DATA – Periodische Datenübertragung
docs.text:
Diese Funktion dient dazu, alle Werte – sowohl proportionale als auch digitale Funktionen – periodisch zu setzen.

Startbyte Tag der Datenstruktur Länge Wert (Daten) Prüfsumme 0xA2 entsprechender Tag 0x05 0x87 0x80 0x00 0x00 0x00 0xCF

docs.title: Multiswitch-Systeme (Futaba, Graupner)
docs.text:
Multiswitch-Systeme sind langsamer, da die Signale multiplexartig nacheinander übertragen werden und hauptsächlich Knöpfe oder Schalter (keine Sticks) verwenden. Daher können die Zustände „short“ und „half“ nicht verwendet werden.

CH Memory Short Memory Long Permanent Top Full Top Half Bottom Half Bottom Full CH1 Memory Short Memory Long Permanent Top Full Top Half Bottom Half Bottom Full CH2 Memory Short Memory Long Permanent Top Full Top Half Bottom Half Bottom Full … CHx Memory Short Memory Long Permanent Top Full Top Half Bottom Half Bottom Full

docs.title: SUMD und SUMDV3
docs.text:
Ob SUMD oder SUMDV3 verwendet wird, hängt von der Version des Senders/Empfängers und der Firmware des Empfängers ab.

  • SUMD: Funktioniert analog zu IBUS, SBUS, EX-Bus und CPPM (siehe oben).
  • SUMDV3: Überträgt ein Feld sogenannter „digitaler Schalter“, die von RCMultiBus-Modulen empfangen und gespeichert werden. Hier können sowohl kanalspezifische Schalter (wie bei IBUS, SBUS, EX-Bus, CPPM) als auch SUMDV3-spezifische digitale Schalter verwendet werden.

docs.title: PWM Standard
docs.text: Funktioniert ähnlich wie IBUS, SBUS, EX-Bus und CPPM, allerdings mit nur einem Kanal (CH1).

docs.title: UART-Einstellungen
docs.text:
• Aktuelle Einstellung: 38.400 Baud, 8 Bytes, LSB zuerst, keine Parität, 1 Stoppbit, Timeout 100 µs
  • Zukünftig geplant: 100.000 Baud

docs.title: Zentrale zeitgesteuerte Funktionen
docs.text:
Zentrale zeitgesteuerte Funktionen wie Blinker werden durch zwei digitale Funktionen gesteuert:

  • Information, ob die Funktion generell ein- oder ausgeschaltet ist
  • Impuls, der die Lampe an- und ausschaltet

Um den Blinker zu steuern, reicht es aus, die Impulsinformation zu setzen. Die andere Information kann für Mixer und weitere Schalter verwendet werden, z. B. um bei eingeschaltetem Blinker auf eine zweite Konfiguration umzuschalten.

docs.title: Basisfunktionen (Tag 0x01)
docs.text:
Die Basisfunktionen sind in 5 Bytes codiert:

Offset Bit 7 Bit 6 Bit 5 Bit 4 Bit 3 Bit 2 Bit 1 Bit 0 0 Warnblinker Blinker links Blinker rechts Fernlichtblitzer Nebelscheinwerfer Fernlicht Abblendlicht Standlicht 1 Stützbeine hoch Fünfte Rad Motor läuft Anlasser Hupe Hecknebellicht Bremse Rückwärtsgang 2 Blinker rechts Impuls Rundumleuchte Tagfahrlicht Blinker links Senken Heben Stützbeine runter 3 Intern/RFU Signalhupe Gang runter Gang rauf Kurvenlicht links Kurvenlicht rechts Intern/RFU Blinker links Impuls 4 Servo 2 Servo 1 Gang 2/2 Gang 1/2 Pad Level Rampe runter Rampe hoch Intern/RFU

docs.title: 01 RS1
docs.text: by Wolfgang · May 16, 2026

docs.title: RS1 gets stuck in a startup loop.
docs.text:
If the RS1 restarts continuously in a loop, it is usually due to very old firmware combined with a defective SD card.

Solution steps

  1. Replace the SD card (max. 32 GB, FAT32 recommended)
  2. Install the latest version of the ControlPanel
  3. Copy contents from Dokumente\KraftwerK\ControlPanel\sdcard_original to the new SD card
  4. Restart the RS1
  5. Restart the ControlPanel
  6. Perform all available updates

docs.title: RS1 aborts updates – why?
docs.text:
If an update does not complete, the firmware directory is often missing on the SD card.

How to fix the problem

  1. Open the SD card directly on the PC or in the ControlPanel under Sound Settings → Open SD Card
  2. Check if a folder named Firmware exists on the SD card
  3. If not present: create a new folder named Firmware
  4. Restart the RS1
  5. Restart the ControlPanel

docs.title: Premium sounds cannot be installed – what’s going on?
docs.text:
There is currently a server issue preventing the loading and activation of premium sounds.

Status

  • The problem is known
  • Active work is underway to fix it
  • An upcoming ControlPanel version will resolve the error

docs.title: 02 ControlPanel
docs.text: by Wolfgang · May 16, 2026

docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions – ControlPanel
docs.text: by Wolfgang · May 16, 2026

docs.title: How do I install the ControlPanel?
docs.text:
In the kraftwerk.shop Service Center you will find the current Downloads.

  1. Download the file
  2. Run it
  3. If Windows shows a security warning: choose "Run anyway" (the link is sometimes a bit hidden)
  4. Complete the installation as usual

After installation, the ControlPanel starts automatically and usually detects connected modules immediately.

docs.title: The installation starts every time for me.
docs.text:
Please do not start the ControlPanel via the downloaded file. This file is the installer – and therefore runs the installation again every time.

Instead, open the ControlPanel via:

  • the Start menu, or
  • the desktop shortcut

docs.title: The system settings are not displayed.
docs.text: On some computers, display issues may occur. In many cases, simply resizing the window helps, as this causes the interface to be rebuilt.

docs.title: EasyBus Lighting Systems Manual
docs.text: Wolfgang Haring e.U. Company Register Number: FN 312421 v (Regional Court Wr. Neustadt) Kammanngasse 7-9A/8, A-2700 Wiener Neustadt Phone: +49 30 6098490 431

docs.title: Welcome
docs.text:
Dear Customer,

We are very pleased that you have chosen one of our products. If you have any questions or suggestions, please do not hesitate to contact us by email at info@kraftwerk.shop or visit our website www.kraftwerk.shop. There you will always find the latest user manuals, software updates, and video tutorials.

We wish you much enjoyment with your new product!

docs.title: Safety Instructions
docs.text:
• Read this manual carefully and pay special attention to the warnings marked in red.
  • Changes to the wiring may only be made when the device is switched off.
  • Ensure that no unexpected movements (e.g., drive wheels) can cause damage during commissioning or configuration – ideally, prop up the model.
  • Protect the electronics from moisture and oil.
  • Do not route data-carrying cables (antenna, bus cables) parallel to power cables (motor, battery, digital servos) to avoid interference.
  • Ensure a stable and sufficient power supply. Cheap BECs or speed controllers with "BEC 5V/1A" specifications often do not provide the necessary voltage quality.
  • Replace damaged components and cables immediately.
  • Operate the components only within the specified technical specifications.
  • Disconnect the battery after driving, especially important for LiPo batteries.
  • Avoid short circuits and reverse polarity.

docs.title: General
docs.text:
Model building is becoming increasingly demanding, and so are the requirements for the electronics. They must be powerful, compact, expandable, configurable, updatable, and above all, easy to use.

We have perfectly implemented these requirements for truck functional model building with the KLM 4/16. This device is not only a standalone lighting system with numerous functions and adjustment options but also the control center of our powerful EasyBus systems.

Pre-assembled lighting boards can be connected via the EasyBus, which are equipped with wired LEDs for side indicators and marker lights depending on the model.

No PC is necessary for commissioning: simply plug in the boards, learn the channels, and get started – plug & play!

If you want to make adjustments (e.g., LED brightness, on/off speeds, blink frequency, brake light sensitivity), connect the system to the PC via USB. Our Kraftwerk ControlPanel automatically detects all connected modules and displays them.

Operation is intuitive with the mouse via selection lists, checkboxes, and percentage values. Changes are saved directly in the respective module. This allows models and trailers to be easily exchanged with colleagues. Settings can be saved and restored on the PC. A "Factory Settings" button resets modules if needed.

The live data view shows in real time what is happening in the model, conveying the flair of the automotive upper class.

Our EasyBus systems are more than lighting systems: the range also includes universal switch expansions, servo controls, speed controllers, as well as infrared transmitters and receivers.

docs.title: Product Description
docs.text:
The KLM 4/16 is connected to up to four channels of the receiver:

  • Throttle channel for controlling reverse and brake lights
  • Steering channel for cornering lights and automatic turn signal reset
  • Up to two additional channels for lighting and auxiliary functions

Alternatively, the KLM 4/16 can be controlled via CPPM, I-BUS, or S-BUS (if supported by the receiver) or a data interface.

Of the 16 switching outputs, eight are high-current outputs (up to 500 mA), the other eight have 180-ohm resistors and can directly drive LEDs.

Each output can be freely configured in function (e.g., parking light, brake light, turn signal) and behavior (switch-on/off speed, brightness). Output 8 can be configured as an infrared transmitter (transmitting diode included).

The four servo outputs can also be adjusted in rotation direction, end positions, and speed.

The KLM 4/16 is compatible with all speed controllers, sound modules, and remote control systems. It supports multiswitch modules from Graupner and Robbe as well as our control pads basic and additional functions and the new Light And Sound Pad.

docs.title: Technical Data KLM 4/16
docs.text: Parameter Value Power supply via receiver power supply (BEC of the speed controller, typically 5-6 V) Max. current consumption 30 mA Voltage at the "+" outputs BEC voltage (typically 5-6 V) Number of servo inputs 4 Number of analog inputs 6 Alternative inputs CPPM, I-BUS, S-BUS, SUMD Supported multiswitches ROBBE, Graupner Supported speed controllers All Supported sound modules All Supported remote control systems All Number of servo outputs 4 (adjustable) Number of switching outputs 16 (adjustable) Current load capacity outputs 1-8 500 mA each for 30 seconds Current load capacity outputs 9-16 20 mA each (180 ohm resistors integrated) Dimensions 52 x 36 x 13 mm

docs.title: Commissioning
docs.text: No special steps are required for commissioning the KraftwerK EasyBus systems. However, we recommend observing the following checklist.

docs.title: Connection to the Receiver
docs.text:
image6.png

Before connecting the KLM 4/16 to the receiver, please check the assignment of the receiver outputs. Use a servo and test each output for correct function. Note the assignment.

image7.jpeg

Note: To prevent unintended movement, it is advisable to prop up the model.

Connect the servo cables for steering and throttle to the corresponding receiver outputs. The speed controller and steering servo are connected to the servo outputs of the KLM. Channels K1 and K2 can be used optionally.

Tip: The connection of throttle and/or steering channel can be omitted if brake, reverse light, and/or indicators are fed in analog (see chapter 11.5 “Actros 3363 Brake/Reverse Light and Indicators from the Speed Controller”).

Tip: Speed controller and/or steering servo can also be connected directly to the receiver with Y-cables to exclude interference from the KLM.

docs.title: Connection of the Lighting Boards
docs.text: All expansion or lighting boards can be connected directly or via distribution boards to the bus outputs of the light assistant.

docs.title: Learning the Channels
docs.text:
image6.png

Learning the channels is the most important step for the proper operation of the KraftwerK EasyBus systems!

After every change of the settings of your remote control system (throws, directions of rotation, center positions), the channels must be relearned.

Servos and motors controlled by KraftwerK systems must be adjusted via the ControlPanel – not via the menu of the remote control system!

  1. Switch on the remote control. If the drive motor is connected, prop up the model so that no wheels touch the ground.
  2. Switch on the model. After the green LED flashes twice, move the throttle and steering sticks each to the end position for five seconds until the red LED lights up for about one second.
  3. Return the sticks to the center position.

The LED blinks according to the channels:

  • 1x short: Throttle stick – full throttle, then full brake
  • 2x short: Steering stick – full left, then full right
  • 3x short: Additional channel K1 – full left, then full right
  • 4x short: Additional channel K2 – full up, then full down

When all sticks are in the center position, the red LED lights up steadily and signals the end of the learning process. The system restarts and indicates readiness for operation with a double green flash.

docs.title: Light Functions
docs.text: Function Description Image Left/Right Turn Signal Automatic reset, optionally automatic activation when steering is turned (see chapter 9.3 "Steering Type"). Blinking frequency adjustable.   image8.png Hazard Lights Automatically activated when reversing. Parking Light   image9.png Parking Light & Low Beam Optionally with xenon effect for the low beam.   image10.png Parking Light & High Beam Optionally with xenon effect for the high beam.   image11.png Parking Light, Low & High Beam Optionally with xenon effect for low or high beam.   image12.png Low Beam Optionally with xenon effect.   image13.png Low Beam, High Beam Optionally with xenon effect for low or high beam.   image14.png High Beam Optionally with xenon effect.   image15.png Fog Lights Expert: Front lights, all combinations possible.   image16.png     image17.png Daytime Running Lights Always on, can be dimmed when turn signals and/or parking lights are on.   image20.png Cornering Light / Curve Light Active when turn signal is activated (vehicle side adjustable to left). Curve light activates when steering angle exceeds threshold. Optional fog lights with adjustable on/off speeds and afterglow time.   image21.png Brake Light Intelligent control: switches on already during deceleration. Sensitivity and afterglow adjustable. Reverse Light Simple forward/reverse controller or with separate brake/reverse. Cruise control possible in reverse gear. Tail Light, Rear Fog Light All combinations possible. American Turn Signal Parking light and turn signal via one LED.   image22.png American Tail Light Parking light, turn signal, and brake light via one LED. All-Round Light On/Off Switches output to 100% when all-round light is activated. Simple Switch Switches output when the specified function is activated.   image23.png     image24.png Double Switch Switches output to brightness 1 or 2 depending on selected function. Expert: Multi-Flasher Configurable: start delay, number of pulses, pulse duration, pauses. Expert: Running Light Running light configurable over multiple outputs. Expert: KnightRider Well-known KnightRider effect over multiple outputs with adjustable brightness and on/off speed.

docs.title: Servo Functions
docs.text: Function Description Proportional Control of a servo or motor controller with a proportional channel. Proportional Switched Control with proportional channel, active only with a certain switch function (e.g., disable steering when lift axle is raised). Two Positions Servo moves from position 1 to position 2 when the switch function is activated. Three Positions Servo moves from position 2 to position 1 or 3 depending on the activated switch function. Fifth Wheel Plate Control of the fifth wheel plate (manual gearbox).

docs.title: Motor Functions
docs.text: Function Description Proportional Control of a motor with a proportional channel. Proportional Switched Motor control only when the switch function is activated. Drop Legs Motor control when selecting the drop legs function. Dump Bed Motor control when selecting the dump bed function. Tipper Motor control when selecting the tipping function.

docs.title: Control Variants / Receiver Type
docs.text: The control type can be selected in the system settings of the KLM 4/16 under "Receiver Type".

docs.title: Normal 4xPWM
docs.text: The KLM 4/16 is connected to up to four channels of the receiver. The channels must be manually or with the ControlPanel trained to define center position, end points, and rotation directions.

docs.title: CPPM
docs.text:
If your receiver offers a CPPM output (summary signal), it can be connected to the CPPM input of the KLM. If necessary, activate the CPPM output on the receiver (see the remote control system manual).

The channel assignment is done via the LiveData view by moving the sticks one after another and assigning the corresponding channels.

After the assignment, the channels must be trained as usual.

docs.title: I-BUS
docs.text: For receivers with I-BUS output (e.g. Turnigy), the same procedure applies as for CPPM.

docs.title: S-BUS
docs.text: For receivers with S-BUS output (e.g. FUTABA), the same procedure applies as for CPPM.

docs.title: Control Variants / Receiver Types
docs.text: The control type can be selected in the system settings of the KLM 4/16 under "Receiver Type".

docs.title: Normal 4xPWM
docs.text: The KLM 4/16 is connected to up to four channels of the receiver. The channels must be manually or via the ControlPanel learned to define center positions, end points, and rotation directions.

docs.title: CPPM
docs.text: If your receiver offers a CPPM output (summary signal), it can be connected to the CPPM input of the KLM. Activate the CPPM output on the receiver if necessary (see the remote control system manual). The channel assignment is done via the live data view in the ControlPanel.

docs.title: I-BUS
docs.text: For receivers with an I-BUS output (e.g., Turnigy), this can be connected to the I-BUS input of the KLM. Activation on the receiver is required. The further steps correspond to those of CPPM.

docs.title: S-BUS
docs.text: For receivers with an S-BUS output (e.g., FUTABA), the same procedure applies as with CPPM and I-BUS.

docs.title: Speed Controller Type
docs.text:
Basically, every speed controller is compatible with EasyBus. For correct brake and reverse light control, the correct speed controller type must be set:

  • Simple: Reverse drive starts immediately when the stick is pulled backward.
  • Brake Separate: Brake first, then reverse drive after pulling again.
  • Standard Cruise Control: Deceleration transitions directly into reverse drive.
  • Servonaut Cruise Control: Gear shifting via K1, set servo output to "Proportional function K1".
  • Servonaut Neutral: Like Servonaut Cruise Control, but with neutral position between forward and reverse.
  • Servonaut K40: Brake and reverse light switched in K40 style.
  • Analog Brake/Reverse Light: Switch outputs of a speed controller fed in analog (see chapter 11.4).

docs.title: Intelligent Brake Light
docs.text: The brake light switches already during deceleration, not only during reverse driving. Sensitivity and afterglow duration can be adjusted in the system settings.

docs.title: Steering Type
docs.text: By default, the turn signal must be switched on manually and is automatically reset. Alternatively, the "Automatic Turn Signal" can be selected, which is automatically activated during cornering. The "Turn Signal Threshold" defines the activation threshold.

docs.title: Cornering Light Type
docs.text: The cornering light behavior can be set via the parameter "Cornering Light Type". The options are self-explanatory.

docs.title: Sound Module
docs.text: Sound modules can be operated directly on the receiver. For control via EasyBus, they must be connected to two servo outputs (directly on the KLM 4/16 or on KSB 2/4).

docs.title: Servonaut SMT Compatible
docs.text:
One channel for throttle information, the second for starting/stopping and horn (keyed in opposite directions).

image30.png

docs.title: Programmable Sound Modules (e.g. BEIER USM-RC)
docs.text:
Based on the Servonaut mode, but extend the possibility to control a second horn.

image31.png

The following settings must be made in the sound module:

image32.png

docs.title: Control of the Sound Module
docs.text:
No further settings are required with the Light & Sound control pad. The horn button controls the horn, the level button starts or stops the engine.

The additional channels K1 and K2 (or up to K10 when using IBUS, SBUS, SUMD, etc.) can be custom assigned in the system settings.

image33.png

A mix of control pad and custom assignment is also possible.

image34.png

docs.title: Important Settings
docs.text:
• Engine starts immediately: Depending on whether the sound module starts the engine immediately, the system setting "Engine starts immediately [active]" must be set or removed.
  • Caution: The drive motor is active in any case!
  • Lights flicker: Each lighting board and switch extension (KLB) has system settings for "Start effect" (flickering at start) and "Engine stop light reduction" (dimming when engine stops). Use the latter only if no components with integrated electronics are controlled.
  • The duration of the flickering can be adjusted with "Engine start duration" to match the sound module start duration.

docs.title: Connection Diagrams
docs.text:
• Actros 3363 with standard cruise control

image36.png - Actros 3363 with standard cruise control and infrared connection for the trailer

image37.png - Actros 3363 with cruise control

image38.png - Actros 3363 brake/reverse light from the cruise control

image39.png - Actros 3363 brake/reverse light and turn signal from the cruise control

image40.png

docs.title: Engine Starts Immediately
docs.text:
Depending on the sound module, the system setting "Engine starts immediately [active]" must be adjusted:

  • Sound module starts engine immediately: check the box.
  • Engine must be started manually: uncheck the box.

Caution: The drive motor is active in any case!

docs.title: Lights Flicker
docs.text:
Each lighting board and switch extension (KLB) has system settings for "Start effect" (flickering at start) and "Motor stop light reduction" (dimming when motor stops). Use the latter option only if no components with integrated electronics are connected.

The duration of the flickering can be adjusted with "Engine start duration" to match the start sound duration.

docs.title: Settings
docs.text:
Our EasyBus systems offer two types of settings:

  • System settings: Apply to the entire module or system (e.g., blink interval, receiver type, brake light type).
  • Output settings: Apply only to the respective output (see Chapter 7 "Functions").

docs.title: Open system settings
docs.text:
Via the gear icon or the menu "System" → "System settings".

image41.png

image42.png

Name Description Start delay [s] Delays system start to account for startup times of remote controls. Learning mode [active] Activates/deactivates manual learning mode via throttle and steering stick. ControlPanel learning remains active. Infrared [on] Switches output 8 to infrared operation. Blinker On/Off Defines blink interval in 10 ms steps (e.g., 500 ms = 0.5 s). Receiver type See Chapter 8 "Control variants / Receiver type". Speed controller type See Chapter 9.1 "Speed controller type". Hazard lights when reversing Automatic activation of hazard lights when reversing. Brake light sensitivity See Chapter 9.2 "Intelligent brake light". Brake light afterglow See Chapter 9.2 "Intelligent brake light". Steering type See Chapter 9.3 "Steering type". Blinker threshold See Chapter 9.3 "Steering type". Cornering light type See Chapter 0 "Cornering light type". Engine runs immediately [active] See Chapter 10 "Sound module". Engine start duration See Chapter 10 "Sound module". Pad All On/Off Configures behavior of the "All On/Off" button on control pads. Pad Light&Sound High beam See control pad Light & Sound manual. Pad Light&Sound Fog lights See control pad Light & Sound manual. Assignment K1 – K12 See Chapter 13.6 "User defined".

docs.title: System Settings
docs.text:
System settings apply to the entire module or system (e.g., blink interval, receiver type, brake light type). You open these via the gear icon or the menu “System” → “System Settings”.

image41.png

image42.png

Name Description Start Delay [s] Delays system start to account for startup times of the remote control. Learning Mode [active] Activates/deactivates manual learning mode (throttle and steering sticks at end positions). ControlPanel learning remains active. Infrared [on] Switches output 8 to infrared mode. Blinker On/Off Defines on and off time of the blinker in 10 ms steps. Receiver Type See section 8 “Control Variants / Receiver Type”. Speed Controller Type See section 9.1 “Speed Controller Type”. Hazard Lights When Reversing Automatic activation of hazard lights when reversing. Brake Light Sensitivity Sensitivity of the intelligent brake light. Brake Light Afterglow Duration of brake light afterglow. Steering Type See section 9.3 “Steering Type”. Blinker Threshold Threshold for automatic blinker activation. Cornering Light Type See section 0 “Cornering Light Type”. Engine Runs Immediately [active] See section 10 “Sound Module”. Engine Start Duration Duration of engine start flickering. Pad All On/Off Configuration of the “All On/Off” button of the control pads. Pad Light & Sound High Beam / Fog Lights See control pad light & sound manual. Assignment K1 – K12 See section 13.6 “User Defined”.

docs.title: Control of Functions Light – Driving – Additional
docs.text:
The standard control is via four proportional channels (throttle left, steering right). Channel K2 switches between the function groups Light, Driving, and Additional.

image43.png

Learning the channels:

  1. Throttle – Brake
  2. Left – Right
  3. K1 left – right
  4. K2 up – down

image44.png

docs.title: Switching Light Functions
docs.text:
• Parking light, low beam: K2 up + tap K1 briefly left
  • High beam: K2 up + tap K1 briefly right (only if parking light is on)
  • Fog lights, rear fog light: K2 up + hold K1 long right (only if parking light is on)
  • Blinker left/right: K2 middle + tap K1 briefly left/right
  • Hazard lights: K2 middle + hold K1 long right
  • Flash-to-pass: K2 middle + hold K1 long left (after first activation, K1 left can be controlled directly)
  • Additional functions: K2 down + move K1 to end position (left/right) briefly or long

image45.png

to

image54.png

docs.title: Pad Basic or Additional Functions
docs.text:
Pads encode button presses into upward and downward movements. The KLM decodes these and controls the functions. Pads are connected to the remote transmitter and connected to K1 or K2 of the KLM.

To learn the channels, the pad must be put into setup mode (simultaneous pressing of the two upper right setup buttons). Alternatively, use the “Learn Channels” assistant in the ControlPanel.

image61.png

image63.png

docs.title: Pad Light & Sound
docs.text:
Controls functions depending on button press with deflections from +100 to -100%. Connection and operation analogous to basic and additional functions.

image65.png

Functions are divided into short, long, and additional layers. Flash-to-pass, horn, and other functions are activated by long press and can then be controlled with short presses.

After two seconds of inactivity, the original assignment is restored.

image67.png

docs.title: Multiswitch Robbe/Graupner
docs.text:
Both protocols are detected automatically. Multiswitch values can be assigned to channels K3 to K12 in the Live Data assistant and user-defined.

image68.png

docs.title: User Defined
docs.text:
Each channel (except throttle and steering) can be assigned individually. K1 and K2 support full and half deflections, K3 to K12 only full deflections.

image69.png

image70.png

Functions can be defined as “Continuous”, “Memory short”, or “Memory long”. Examples include light functions, horn, fifth wheel coupling, supports, dump body, ramp, start, shift, servo1/2, etc.

image71.png

docs.title: Control of Functions Light – Driving – Additional
docs.text: The standard control is via four proportional channels (Throttle left, Steering right). Channel K2 switches between the groups Light, Driving, and Additional functions.

docs.title: Learning the Channels
docs.text:
1. Throttle – Brake
  2. Left – Right
  3. K1 left – right
  4. K2 up – down

image44.png

docs.title: Switching Light Functions
docs.text:
• Parking light, low beam: K2 up + tap K1 briefly left
  • High beam: K2 up + tap K1 briefly right (only when parking light is on)
  • Fog lights, rear fog light: K2 up + tap K1 long right (only when parking light is on)
  • Turn signals left/right: K2 middle + tap K1 briefly left/right
  • Hazard lights: K2 middle + tap K1 long right
  • Flash-to-pass: K2 middle + tap K1 long left (then K1 left for On/Off)
  • Additional functions (full/half, short/long): K2 down + move K1 accordingly

image45.png

to

image54.png

docs.title: Driving – Light – Additional (Alternative Control)
docs.text:
Analogous to the standard version, with the same control commands.

image55.png

to

docs.title: General
docs.text:
When starting the ControlPanel, the model must be operational: battery charged and connected, model and remote control switched on and bound, servos movable.

The system is automatically detected and all modules are displayed.

image72.png

image73.png

docs.title: Module Overview
docs.text:
By clicking on a module, you reach the output overview. There you can assign names, set on and off speeds, and configure additional parameters depending on the function.

Save changes with the floppy disk icon. Help is available via the question mark. With the eye icon, outputs on the model can be activated directly to facilitate assignment.

image74.png

docs.title: Dual Assignment
docs.text:
Each output can be assigned twice. Example:

  • First level: parking light & high beam
  • Second level: hazard warning lights

Switching between levels is configurable.

docs.title: Live Data
docs.text:
The live data view shows stick positions and switch states in real time. Channels can be learned and functions switched on or off.

image86.png

[[IMAGE_078]] When the channels are learned correctly, the blue dots must follow your stick movements. If you use CPPM, IBUS, or SBUS, the channels must also be assigned correctly. 665544332211 [[IMAGE_079]] All channels are displayed here as actually measured. The learning information is not considered here, i.e., the bars can move down even if the stick is moved up. 1: A change of channel assignment should only be done if CPPM, IBUS, or SBUS is used. 2-5: These values are set by the learning process. 1000 – 2000 and 1500 center position are the standard values. The values can be adjusted and saved. 6: Scroll right to see the values for other channels (only relevant for CPPM, IBUS, SBUS) The right side of the live data shows the switch states. Some can be set by clicking. The three function groups basic light functions, additional functions, and analog can be used as switches in the functions, e.g., in the simple switch function. [[IMAGE_080]] [[IMAGE_081]]

image87.png

With correct learning, the blue dots follow the stick movements. For CPPM, I-BUS, or S-BUS, the channel assignment must be correct.

image88.png

The right side shows switch states, some of which can be changed by clicking.

docs.title: Updates
docs.text:
After connecting, the ControlPanel shows available updates for bus modules.

image6.png

Tips for a safe update:

  • Update only one module at a time.
  • Avoid power interruptions.
  • Disconnect servos and motors during the update.
  • Restart model and ControlPanel if problems occur.

image92.png

image6.png

Updates can reset factory settings. The ControlPanel automatically backs up all settings on the hard drive.

image93.png

image94.png

image95.png

After updates, all modules are displayed. The KLM 4/16 consists of the main module with four servo outputs and the integrated switch extension with 16 switch outputs.

image73.png

docs.title: Device Information
docs.text:
Displays basic information about modules. "Show Device" switches on the first three outputs, helpful with multiple extensions without name assignment.

Modules can be restarted, reset to factory settings, or updated.

image96.png

docs.title: System Analysis
docs.text:
Helps with troubleshooting by reading out information, searching for damaged modules, and communication tests.

image97.png

image6.png

docs.title: Channel Learning
docs.text:
The assistant supports channel learning. Set the correct assignment for K1 and K2 beforehand, especially when using control pads.

image98.png

docs.title: Pad Basic or Additional Functions
docs.text:
Pads encode key presses into two upward and downward movements. The KLM decodes these and controls the functions accordingly.

The pad is connected to a control device in the remote control transmitter, the channel to K1 or K2 of the KLM.

To learn the channels, the pad must be set to setup mode (press both setup buttons simultaneously). Alternatively, the "Learn Channels" assistant in the ControlPanel is recommended.

image61.png

image63.png

docs.title: Pad Light & Sound
docs.text:
Controls functions depending on key press with deflections from +100 to -100%. Connection as with basic/additional functions.

image65.png

Functions for short and long presses are color-coded and symbolically marked.

Special features: Some keys (e.g. headlight flasher, horn, supports) are activated by a long press and can then be controlled with short presses without triggering the short assignment. After two seconds of inactivity, the original assignment is restored.

Example: Press and hold the headlight flasher button → headlight flasher on, release → headlight flasher off, short presses toggle the headlight flasher on/off, after two seconds without pressing the mode resets.

The button for the additional layer switches to a second function layer, which is automatically reset after two seconds.

The behavior of the "All On/All Off" button can be configured in the system settings.

image67.png

The Servo 1/2 buttons do not directly control servos but switching states that can be used on the servo outputs.

docs.title: General
docs.text:
• Turn signals and cornering lights respond reversed: Channels are not learned.
  • Reverse light stays on when driving forward: Channels are not learned.
  • Steering servo rotates incorrectly: Correct rotation direction after learning with ControlPanel. Changes in the transmitter are overwritten during learning.
  • Light functions cannot be controlled: Check if the model is operational (battery charged, transmitter and model switched on). Check wiring. Test the speed controller directly on the receiver without the light assistant.
  • No response after switching on: Green LED should blink twice briefly. Channels must be learned.
  • Learning channels not possible: Check connection and function of all channels with a servo. Use the LiveData assistant to verify channel movements.
  • No connection to PC: Check driver installation, model switched on and USB connected, correct COM port selected.
  • Module not found after update: Close ControlPanel, restart system, try update again. If problems persist, disconnect all modules except the affected one.

docs.title: Updates
docs.text:
After connecting to the ControlPanel, available updates for bus modules are offered.

image6.png

Tips for a safe update:

  • Always connect only one module and restart the ControlPanel.
  • Avoid power interruptions during the update.
  • Disconnect servos or motors during the update to avoid interference.
  • Regularly restart the model and ControlPanel, especially during unusually long operations.

image92.png

image6.png

Updates can reset factory settings. The ControlPanel automatically saves all settings on the hard drive so they can be restored later.

image93.png

image94.png

image95.png

After updates, all modules are displayed. The KLM 4/16 consists of the main module with four servo outputs and the integrated switch extension with 16 switch outputs.

docs.title: Terms
docs.text:
• ControlPanel: Free PC software for system configuration.
  • Control pad: Pads mounted on the remote control transmitter for convenient operation.
  • Parameter: Changeable values and settings in the system.

docs.title: Device Information
docs.text:
Here you will find basic information about the modules. "Show Device" turns on the first three outputs to facilitate identification.

With "Reset" you restart the module, "Factory Settings" resets it, and "Update" forces an update.

If updates are available, you will be informed when the ControlPanel starts. Interrupted updates can be manually resumed here.

Every channel except throttle and steering can be custom assigned. K1 and K2 with full and half throws, K3 to K12 only with full throws.

"Duration" means the function remains active as long as the stick is in the position. "Memory short" and "Memory long" are for short- and long-pressed functions.

Available functions include lighting functions, horn, supports, tipper, ramp, starting, gear shifting, servo1/2, and much more.

The learning information is not considered here, i.e., the bars can also move downwards even if the stick is moved upwards. 1: A change of channel assignment should only be done if CPPM, IBUS, or SBUS is used. 2-5: These values are set by the learning process. 1000 – 2000 and 1500 center position are the standard values. The values can be adjusted and saved. 6: Scroll right to see the values for the other channels (only relevant for CPPM, IBUS, SBUS) The right side of the live data shows the switch states. Some can be set by clicking. The three function groups basic light functions, additional functions, and analog can be used as switches in the functions, e.g., in the simple switch function. [[IMAGE_080]] [[IMAGE_081]]

image87.png

The blue dots must follow the stick movements. For CPPM, IBUS, or SBUS, channels must be correctly assigned.

image88.png

The right side shows switch states, which can also be set by clicking. The three function groups basic light, additional functions, and analog can be used as switches.

image90.png

image91.png

docs.title: Teaching Channels
docs.text:
The assistant helps you teach the channels. Make sure that K1 and K2 are assigned correctly, especially when using control pads.

image98.png

docs.title: General
docs.text:
• Turn signals and cornering lights respond reversed: Channels are not calibrated.
  • Reverse light is on when driving forward: Channels are not calibrated.
  • Steering servo rotates the wrong way: Adjust the rotation direction after calibration with ControlPanel. Changes in the transmitter will be overwritten during calibration.
  • Lighting functions cannot be controlled: Check if the model is operational (battery charged, transmitter and model turned on). Check wiring and connect the speed controller directly to the receiver to rule out errors.
  • Lighting system operational? Green LED blinks twice briefly after powering on.
  • Channels calibrated? Calibration is necessary to define end positions and rotation directions.
  • Calibration not possible: Check connection and function of all channels with a servo. Use the live data assistant to verify channel movements.
  • No connection to PC: Driver installed? Model turned on and connected via USB? Correct COM port selected?
  • Module not found after update: Close ControlPanel, restart the model (disconnect battery), try the update again.
  • No damaged module displayed: Disconnect all modules from the bus, restart ControlPanel, connect the problematic module and reconnect.

docs.title: Updates
docs.text:
After connecting, the ControlPanel offers available updates for bus modules.

image6.png

Update Tips:

  • Always update only one module at a time to avoid errors.
  • Avoid power interruptions during the update.
  • Disconnect servos/motors if twitching or humming occurs.
  • Restart the model and ControlPanel regularly, especially if there are unusual delays.

image92.png

image6.png

Updates can reset modules to factory settings. The ControlPanel automatically backs up all settings on the hard drive so they can be restored later.

image93.png

image94.png

image95.png

After updates, all modules are displayed. The KLM 4/16 consists of the main module with four servo outputs and the integrated switch extension with 16 switch outputs.

image73.png

docs.title: Terms
docs.text:
• ControlPanel: Free PC software for configuring the system.
  • Control Pad: Pads mounted on the remote control transmitter for convenient control.
  • Parameter: Adjustable values and settings.

docs.title: Device Information
docs.text:
Displays basic information about modules. Using "Show Device" turns on the first three outputs, useful for multiple universal expansions without naming.

"Reset" restarts the module (similar to unplugging and plugging back in). "Factory Settings" resets to factory defaults, "Update" forces an update.

If a new version is available, the ControlPanel indicates this at startup and offers the update. Manual updates are possible via Device Information.

image96.png

docs.title: System Analysis
docs.text:
Helps with troubleshooting by reading out information, searching for damaged modules, and communication testing.

image97.png

image6.png

docs.title: Learn Channels
docs.text:
The assistant supports learning the channels. Make sure that K1 and K2 are assigned correctly, especially when using control pads.

image98.png

docs.title: General
docs.text:
• Turn signals and cornering lights respond reversed: Channels are not learned.
  • Reverse light turns on when driving forward: Channels are not learned.
  • Steering servo turns incorrectly: Adjust direction after learning with ControlPanel. Changes in the transmitter after learning neutralize the setting.
  • Light functions cannot be controlled: Check if the model is operational (battery charged, transmitter and model switched on). Check the wiring. Test the speed controller directly on the receiver without the light assistant.
  • Lighting system shows no reaction: The green LED of the light assistant should blink twice briefly after switching on. Channels must be learned.
  • Learning the channels not possible: Check connection and function of the receiver outputs with a servo. Open LiveData Assistant and check if each channel responds cleanly. Start the learning assistant and follow the instructions.
  • No connection to PC: Driver installed? Model switched on and connected via USB? COM port correctly selected?
  • Module not found after update: Close ControlPanel, restart model (disconnect battery, wait briefly, reconnect). Start ControlPanel and check output. If "Damaged module" appears, perform update. In case of problems, disconnect all modules except the affected one and repeat update.
  • No damaged module displayed: Disconnect all modules from the bus, close ControlPanel, restart model, start ControlPanel, connect and connect the problematic module. Perform update or contact support.
